title: rubus suffultus
intro: Rubus suffultus, also known as the Pacific thimbleberry, is a species of thimbleberry that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus suffultus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy that can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
